<<Course Structure and How to Get the Most Out of It>>

*highlighted section*  
**1. Understand the Course Layout**  
Most courses follow a structured format, often including modules, weekly lessons, readings, and assessments. To get the most out of it, start by reviewing the syllabus. Look for [start date], [end date], and [assignment deadlines]. This will help you plan your time effectively. For example, if your course runs from [month] to [month], you can allocate specific days for [homework] and [review sessions].

*highlighted section*  
**2. Prioritize Active Learning**  
Instead of passively reading or watching lectures, engage with the material actively. Take notes, ask questions, and participate in discussions. Many courses offer [discussion forums] or [live sessions] where you can interact with [instructor] and [peers]. For instance, if you're unsure about [topic], don’t hesitate to reach out to [mentor] or join [study group].

*highlighted section*  
**3. Track Your Progress and Adjust**  
Regularly assess your performance by reviewing [quizzes], [midterms], and [final projects]. Use this feedback to adjust your study habits. If you're struggling with [concept], consider seeking extra help through [tutoring] or [resource center]. Keeping a [study schedule] can also help you stay on track, especially if you're balancing [work] and [personal life].
